articles.title: Musk’s biggest loyalist became his biggest liability
articles.summary: Shivon Zilis, Musk's longtime advisor and mother of four of his children, testified in the Musk v. Altman trial about her role overseeing his AI portfolio across Tesla, Neuralink, and OpenAI since 2017.

articles.title: Google shuts down Project Mariner
articles.summary: Google has shut down Project Mariner, an experimental web task automation feature launched in December 2024. The technology will be integrated into other Google products.

articles.title: How David Sacks crashed and burned in the White House
articles.summary: David Sacks, the White House AI and Crypto Czar, experienced a significant failure during his tenure in the administration, marking a notable downfall in his government role.

articles.title: Barry Diller trusts Sam Altman. But ‘trust is irrelevant’ as AGI nears, he says.
articles.summary: Barry Diller supports Sam Altman but warns that trust becomes irrelevant as artificial general intelligence approaches, emphasizing the need for safety guardrails.

articles.title: Snap says its $400M deal with Perplexity ‘amicably ended’
articles.summary: Snap and Perplexity amicably ended their $400 million deal that would have integrated Perplexity's AI search engine into Snapchat, announced last November.

articles.title: Is xAI a neocloud now?
articles.summary: xAI's primary focus may shift toward constructing data centers rather than solely training AI models, suggesting a broader infrastructure business strategy.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud amid surging AI application demand.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality without subscription fees or cloud dependency.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative stunt successfully attracted top engineering talent in a competitive hiring market.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal windows have become increasingly important for developers despite predictions of graphical interfaces replacing command-line interfaces. The command line remains superior for many computing tasks compared to mouse-driven interfaces.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting application was compromised in a monthlong supply-chain attack starting April 8, with malicious updates pushed through official developer servers affecting Windows versions 12.5.0.2421-2434.

articles.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
articles.summary: Reddit blocked mobile web access with a mandatory app overlay, forcing users to download the app without option to bypass. The overlay offers no alternative to continue using the mobile website.

articles.title: The Download: seafloor science and military chatbots
articles.summary: Inexpensive submersibles by Orpheus Ocean can explore the deep ocean at lower costs, enabling seafloor mapping and mineral discovery. However, they raise environmental concerns as deep-sea mining companies show interest.

articles.title: The Download: inside the Musk v. Altman trial, and AI for democracy
articles.summary: Elon Musk is suing Sam Altman, alleging he was misled about OpenAI's transition to a for-profit company in a landmark legal trial. The case centers on competing visions for AI development between two industry leaders.

articles.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
articles.summary: AI is becoming the primary interface for forming beliefs and democratic participation, similar to how the printing press and broadcast media reshaped governance. Design choices made now will determine whether AI strengthens or strains democratic institutions.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM advances from V0 to V1 by prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before applying corrections. The update emphasizes accuracy and reliability in model training.

articles.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
articles.summary: The Open ASR Leaderboard introduces Benchmaxxer Repellant to prevent gaming metrics through specialized optimization techniques that exploit evaluation weaknesses.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advances in large language model construction, featuring improved training methods and architecture optimizations for enhanced performance and efficiency.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and its potential impact. The conversation explores upcoming trends and innovations in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and boost creative capabilities for content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft releases an open-source toolkit designed to modernize agricultural practices and improve farming efficiency through AI and technology integration.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google shares five gardening tips using AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features to help plants thrive. These tools provide gardeners with resources to optimize plant care.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners for the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition, supporting creative content exploring AI's potential impact.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced its latest AI updates in April 2026, showcasing new developments and advancements in artificial intelligence technology and capabilities.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. This AI system aims to support clinicians in patient care through collaborative intelligence.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier AI technology for research innovation.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling local updates from global synchronization, improving fault tolerance and training efficiency.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging the company stole from a charity. The trial revealed evidence of settlement discussions and accusations that xAI used OpenAI's models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #243 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's GPT-5.5 with coding improvements and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The episode discusses this alongside Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 developments.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses enterprise AI challenges, focusing on data leverage, cloud versus local compute decisions, and automating data ingestion for meaningful AI results at scale.

articles.title: US government increases AI suppliers and rethinks Anthropic’s role
articles.summary: The US Pentagon expanded its approved AI suppliers to include Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Reflection AI for classified operations. Anthropic faced contract cancellation after refusing unrestricted government use of its technology.

articles.title: Google tests Remy AI agent for Gemini as focus turns to user control
articles.summary: Google is testing Remy, a new AI personal agent for Gemini designed to take actions on users' behalf for work and daily tasks. The tool is currently in staff-only testing with no public release timeline announced.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur during collaborative problem-solving tasks.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-localization, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to handle context windows of up to 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding LLM capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: Claude Code accidentally leaks API keys to public package registries. The AI coding assistant inadvertently publishes sensitive credentials in code repositories.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, representing a major supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: A Groq-Powered Agentic Research Assistant with LangGraph, Tool Calling, Sub-Agents, and Agentic Memory: Lets Built It
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a Groq-powered agentic research assistant using LangGraph with tool calling, sub-agents, and memory. The system leverages Groq's fast inference for multi-step reasoning tasks including web search, file handling, and structured output generation.

articles.title: CopilotKit Introduces Enterprise Intelligence Platform That Gives Agentic Applications Persistent Memory Across Sessions and Devices
articles.summary: CopilotKit launches Enterprise Intelligence Platform enabling agentic applications to maintain persistent memory across sessions and devices, eliminating the need for developers to build custom storage infrastructure from scratch.

articles.title: Google AI Releases Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) Drafters for Gemma 4: Delivering Up to 3x Faster Inference Without Quality Loss
articles.summary: Google released Multi-Token Prediction drafters for Gemma 4, achieving 3x faster inference speed without quality loss. This speculative decoding architecture addresses the memory-bandwidth bottleneck in large language model deployment.

articles.title: I've fully converted to adaptive chargers from fast ones and already feel safer
articles.summary: Adaptive chargers reduce battery wear by maintaining lower charging speeds, making them ideal for overnight charging. The user switched from fast chargers and reports feeling safer with this approach.

articles.title: How I upgraded my Sonos soundbar's audio quality - 3 easy and free methods
articles.summary: Learn three free methods to improve your Sonos soundbar's audio quality. Simple tweaks can significantly enhance your listening experience without additional costs.

articles.title: I've tested several ReMarkable tablets, but its new cheap E Ink tablet had me fooled
articles.summary: ReMarkable's new Paper Pure e-ink tablet offers an affordable option with thoughtful design and features while maintaining the brand's specialized functionality for note-taking and writing.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce varying outputs from identical inputs. Statistical guardrails help ensure consistency and reliability in these unpredictable systems.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents to enable AI systems to dynamically search, reason, and retrieve information. The article explains this concept across three difficulty levels.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, an algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines used in RAG systems.

articles.title: When the Uncertainty Is Bigger Than the Shock: Scenario Modelling for English Local Elections
articles.summary: A scenario analysis study on English local elections explores calibrated uncertainty and historical error, demonstrating that models provide more value by acknowledging uncertainty limits rather than forcing predictions.

articles.title: Beyond Lists: Using Python Deque for Real-Time Sliding Windows
articles.summary: collections.deque outperforms lists for sliding windows and real-time data streams by eliminating costly element shifting. It provides efficient, thread-safe queue operations for high-performance Python applications.

articles.title: Timer-XL: A Long-Context Foundation Model for Time-Series Forecasting
articles.summary: Timer-XL is a decoder-only Transformer foundation model designed for time-series forecasting with long-context capabilities. The article explores its internal mechanisms and architecture.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric powers AI infrastructure for major companies like OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle, offering advanced networking for large-scale AI deployment with superior performance and reliability.

articles.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
articles.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow partner to develop autonomous AI agents for enterprises, moving beyond basic AI capabilities to enable complex task automation with safety and control in real workflows.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents have become a major open-source phenomenon with over 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026, enabling organizations to build specialized AI systems on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: Cost effective deployment of vision-language models for pet behavior detection on AWS Inferentia2
articles.summary: Furbo Pet Camera uses vision-language models to detect pet behavior in real-time. The company reduced inference costs by switching from GPU-based EC2 instances to AWS Inferentia2 chips.

articles.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
articles.summary: Hapag-Lloyd, a major shipping company, uses Amazon Bedrock to analyze customer feedback and convert it into actionable business insights. This AI-powered approach enhances their digital customer experience across their global operations.

articles.title: Streamlining generative AI development with MLflow v3.10 on Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow Apps now supports MLflow v3.10, offering enhanced capabilities for generative AI development, improved experiment tracking, and better observability for data scientists and ML engineers.

articles.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables development of in-vehicle AI agents that shift automotive cockpits from rule-based interfaces to intelligent, multimodal systems capable of reasoning and planning decisions.

articles.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
articles.summary: AI is evolving from simple request-response systems to autonomous agents that dynamically call tools, spawn sub-agents, and manage their own decision-making processes, requiring new co-design approaches.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills optimizes supply chain decisions by using agentic AI to handle fluctuating demand, volatile costs, and capacity constraints. The solution automates complex decision-making traditionally requiring specialized operations research teams.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study explores how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms, examining generational attitudes toward digital advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta committed to achieving net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental impact.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative XR technology by applying unique perspectives and unconventional approaches to the field, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive technological breakthroughs.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and improved gradient signals for vision models.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale, advancing interpretability by analyzing how input features collectively drive model predictions beyond individual attribution.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an information estimator to evaluate measurement quality from noisy images. The approach quantifies how well measurements distinguish between objects using only noisy data and noise models.
